{{基礎情報 公家
| 氏名 = Isachi-no-Sukune
| 画像 =
| 画像サイズ =
| 画像説明 =
| 時代 = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}
| 生誕 = Unknown
| 死没 = Unknown
| 改名 =
| 別名 = Isachi-no-Atai, Isahi-no-Sukune, Unakami-no-Isachi
| 諡号 =
| 神号 =
| 戒名 =
| 墓所 =
| 官位 = First or second {{ill|Kami-tsu-Unakami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上海上国造}}
| 主君 = {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}
| 氏族 = Same ancestor as the {{ill|Izumo-no-Omi|ja|出雲国造|lt=Izumo-no-Omi|lt_ja=出雲臣}}; branch of the {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造}}
| 父母 = {{ill|Etamohi-no-Mikoto|ja|兄多毛比命}}
| 兄弟 = {{ill|Ōshika Kuni-no-Atai|ja|大鹿国直}}, Ana-Yamato-Ko-no-Atai, Ōyagi-no-Atai
| 妻   =
| 子   =
| 特記事項 = Ancestor of the {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉士|lt=Kishi|lt_ja=吉師}} and the {{ill|Naniwa Kishi-be|ja|吉士|lt=Naniwa Kishi-be|lt_ja=難波吉師部}}
}}
'''Isachi-no-Sukune''' (''isachi no sukune'') was a {{ill|gōzoku|ja|豪族}} of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In the Empress Jingū regency chronicle of the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' he is written as '''Isachi-no-Sukune'''; in the ''{{ill|Kokuzō Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}'' as '''Isachi-no-Atai'''; and in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' as '''Isahi-no-Sukune'''.

== Overview ==
In both the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', he appears as the general Isachi-no-Sukune (''isachi no sukune'', Isahi-no-Sukune){{efn|Ancestor of the {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師}}: it is unclear whether this is the same clan as the Naniwa Kishi subordinates who danced the {{ill|Kishi-mai|ja|吉志舞}} together with the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}.}} who, together with {{ill|Kuramiwake-no-Ōkimi|ja|倉見別王}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Takebe clan|ja|建部氏}}, sided with {{ill|Prince Kagosaka|ja|麛坂皇子}} and {{ill|Prince Oshikuma|ja|忍熊皇子}} and fought against {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}. After being defeated in battle, the ''Kojiki'' states that he went out to sea and composed a poem together with Prince Oshikuma before drowning in the sea, while the ''Nihon Shoki'' states that he composed a poem together with Prince Oshikuma and sank at the Seta crossing.

Based on the descriptions in both books, Isachi-no-Sukune is considered to have committed suicide at this point, but in the Empress Jingū regency chronicle of the ''Nihon Shoki'', by Empress Jingū's command, {{ill|Wakahirume-no-Mikoto|ja|稚日女尊}} was enshrined at Ikuta-Nagasa-no-Kuni.

The ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' {{ill|Kokuzō Hongi|ja|国造本紀}} makes him the ancestor of the {{ill|Munasashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|胸刺国造}}.

The "50" (''i'', ''ito'', ''iso'', etc.) part of the name appears in old names connected to the imperial family, but its meaning is unclear.

== Genealogy ==
His father was {{ill|Etamohi-no-Mikoto|ja|兄多毛比命}}, the first {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造}}. His brothers include {{ill|Ōshika Kuni-no-Atai|ja|大鹿国直}}, the first {{ill|Kikuma no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|菊麻国造}}; Ana-Yamato-Ko-no-Atai, the first {{ill|Ōshima no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|大島国造}}; and Ōyagi-no-Sukune, the first {{ill|Hōki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伯岐国造}}.

== Footnotes ==
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
=== Notes ===
{{Notelist}}
=== Citations ===
{{Reflist}}

== See also ==
*{{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}
*{{ill|List of Figures from before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:うなかみ の いさち}}
[[Category:日本神話の人物]]
[[Category:古墳時代の人物]]
[[Category:古代日本の人物]]
[[Category:神功皇后]]
[[Category:神道に関連する人物]]
[[Category:播磨国の人物]]
[[Category:上総国の人物]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:海上五十狭茅]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
[[Category:Pages without wikidata]]
